behaviors.description: The review input is scoped to the lines that changed, not the full content of each modified file.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff (diff extraction -> review-input builder). MOCK-MISS: not a single pure function unless you collapse modules. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required to catch overscoped payload bug; fixture diffs and in-process wiring are enough, so not System. LLM-DEP: assertion is about input scoping, not model judgment, so not Agentic. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text is only about scoping ('lines that changed, not full content'), which can fail before any real API call.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real local diff parser + input-construction modules with a fixture PR patch containing unchanged context; assert produced review payload includes only changed hunks/lines and excludes unchanged file body.

behaviors.description: Inline comments are attached to the correct positions in the diff view on the pull request.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real GitHub diff-position semantics and server-side validation/mapping. MOCK-HIDE: mocked API can accept incorrect position math that real PR diff rejects or renders incorrectly. REAL-INFRA => System (real HTTP + real PR diff behavior). REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior explicitly targets 'correct positions in the diff view on the pull request,' which is external-system truth.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real test PR, post inline comments using computed positions, then fetch PR review comments from GitHub and verify each comment is anchored to the expected file/hunk/line in the actual diff view.

behaviors.description: The agent identifies at least one real defect in a pull request that contains a known introduced bug.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on model output quality (detecting a real defect), which deterministic stubs cannot validate. LLM-DEP => Agentic. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Workflow because behavior asks for one outcome ('identifies at least one real defect'), not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run analysis with the real model on a seeded buggy PR diff corpus and assert at least one returned finding correctly matches the known bug location/category.

behaviors.description: Every finding listed in the top-level summary corresponds to a posted inline comment ID from the same run.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module state consistency (finding tracker + inline-post response IDs + summary assembler). In-process fakes can reproduce mismatched ID propagation. Integration-brake: no required real OS/network behavior, no model-output-quality dependence, no multi-agentic chain. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior focuses on same-run ID linkage, which can fail entirely within local orchestration logic.
behaviors.test_description: Execute posting pipeline with deterministic finding fixtures and stubbed comment-post responses carrying IDs; assert summary payload references only IDs emitted in that run and all referenced IDs exist.

behaviors.description: No inline finding is dropped from the final summary payload before posting.
behaviors.justification: Failure is in module interaction/order (collector -> filter/dedupe -> summary serializer). A unit test of one function misses pipeline drops. Integration-brake: no real OS/network required to expose dropping bug; no dependence on model judgment; no multi-agentic sequence. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text is explicitly 'before posting,' so the critical failure can be reproduced pre-HTTP.
behaviors.test_description: Run local pipeline from inline-finding set through summary payload builder and assert one-to-one preservation (or expected deterministic transformation) of all inline findings in final summary payload.

behaviors.description: The automation sequence (read diff, post inline comments, post summary) succeeds for a real pull request when provided a fixed precomputed finding set.
behaviors.justification: Requires real GitHub API/network and end-to-end sequencing with external side effects. REAL-INFRA => System. LLM output is fixed/precomputed, so Agentic not required. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because behavior explicitly says 'fixed precomputed finding set,' disqualifying model-quality dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Run the automation against a real sandbox PR using fixed findings; verify diff fetch succeeds, inline comments are created, summary comment posts, and all expected artifacts are present via follow-up API reads.

behaviors.description: The full live run with model-generated findings (read diff, analyze, post inline comments, post summary) completes correctly for a real pull request without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: Includes real external infra plus real model-generated findings; correctness depends on actual model decisions, so System alone is insufficient. This is at least Agentic. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Workflow because behavior text shows one agentic stage ('analyze') followed by deterministic posting; it does not require multiple distinct agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute full unattended run on a real sandbox PR using the production model path; assert run completes, produces non-empty valid findings, posts inline comments and summary, and links between generated findings and posted artifacts remain consistent.
